mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed negative returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series signals, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of co-occurring expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term positive equity repricing over the next trading week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in more than 55% of recovery-phase observations, or loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with a low federal funds rate compresses real interest rates, forcing markets to price in aggressive future central bank tightening, which generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The series exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real gross domestic product below 1.0% signals immediate economic stagnation, prompting rapid portfolio deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered quarters, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from random noise with a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and contraction in the unemployment rate confirms synchronized supply and labor market strength, which drives positive equity valuation adjustments over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal underperforms a buy-and-hold strategy by more than 2% annually, or fails to maintain a positive Sharpe ratio in walk-forward tests.
